justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) Request ID gap identified — 'No request ID / correlation ID in any log line'. (2) API call duration gap identified — 'No duration logged for the SmartyStreets API call'. (3) Metric label gap identified — 'validation_requests_total has no result label (valid/invalid/error)'. (4) Latency histogram absence noted — 'No latency histogram (e.g. validation_duration_seconds)'. (5) PII consideration flagged — logged input address noted as a gap but not blindly recommended as a fix; framed as 'even redacted/hashed' showing awareness of sensitivity. (6) Verdict is NOT READY. (7) Recommendations are specific and actionable: 'Add result label to validation_requests_total: {result=valid|invalid|error|timeout}', 'Add Prometheus latency histogram: validation_duration_seconds', 'Add structured logging with correlation ID support'.

observations.text: All four core observability gaps (request ID, duration, metric labels, latency histogram) clearly identified with specific detail
Verdict is correctly NOT READY with clear justification tied to observability gaps
Recommendations are highly specific — names exact metric labels, histogram names, and config changes needed
Goes beyond minimum requirements with excellent resilience analysis (circuit breaker, health check anti-pattern, rate limiting)

PII consideration partially addressed — mentions logging input address 'even redacted/hashed' which acknowledges sensitivity, though could be more explicit about flagging it as a decision point rather than a gap

PII flagging could be stronger — listing 'No input address logged' as a gap implies it should be logged, though the 'even redacted/hashed' qualifier shows some awareness. Ideally would explicitly call out that logging addresses is a PII decision point, not a straightforward recommendation
